title: Peak Span & Cognitive Aging
summary: Peak span — staying within 90% of your peak function across key systems — is a more ambitious target than health span (disease-free) or lifespan (duration). Most capacities peak around age 25 except crystallised intelligence (peaks ~45). Exercise, omega-3, novel learning, and sleep are the primary levers to maintain peak span across all systems simultaneously.

takeaways.headline: Peak span = staying within 90% of your peak — more ambitious than health span
takeaways.point: Three tiers of longevity: lifespan (years lived), health span (disease-free years), and peak span (years spent within 90% of peak function for key capacities). Most capacities peak around age 25: immune function, musculoskeletal strength and bone density, cardiorespiratory fitness (VO2max), and fluid cognitive function (processing speed, novel problem-solving). Crystallised intelligence (knowledge-based reasoning, pattern recognition from accumulated experience) peaks around age 40–45.

takeaways.headline: Aerobic exercise, omega-3, and novel learning are the multi-system peak span levers
takeaways.point: Interventions that maintain peak span across multiple systems simultaneously: (1) Aerobic exercise — increases BDNF, grows neurons, improves brain plasticity, maintains VO2max, supports immune function and musculoskeletal health; (2) Omega-3 — slows epigenetic aging, supports brain function, reduces cancer and cardiovascular mortality; (3) Novel cognitive engagement — learning new things triggers BDNF release, strengthens neural connections, builds cognitive reserve that delays dementia onset; (4) Sleep — critical for immune system aging, memory consolidation, and brain clearance.

takeaways.headline: Outsourcing thinking to AI may build cognitive debt — novelty is the key variable
takeaways.point: Brain connectivity is approximately halved when people outsource writing to AI vs. writing manually (EEG study). 83% of AI users couldn't recall details of text they had written with AI assistance. The mechanism: novelty drives BDNF and neural connection-building. If you're not genuinely engaging with new information — just accepting AI-generated output — you get the surface answer without building the underlying neural architecture. London taxi drivers (who memorise 25,000 streets) have physically enlarged hippocampi and very low Alzheimer's rates.

takeaways.headline: Visceral fat and testosterone decline drive a 200% visceral fat increase between 25–65
takeaways.point: Between ages 25 and 65, men experience approximately a 200% increase in visceral fat even if total body weight stays the same. Testosterone (which helps burn visceral fat) declines ~1%/year from age 30. Women experience an 8–10% annual visceral fat increase starting in perimenopause (typically mid-40s) as estrogen declines. Endocrine-disrupting chemicals (BPA, phthalates, PFAS) accelerate this by lowering testosterone and disrupting estrogen signalling.

sources.title: Dr. Rhonda Patrick on Visceral Fat & Metabolic Health
